Field changes are a notable example: Field records are updated as needed in the field_accum_fact table. For example, when a document is created and extracted, a record for a field on the document has an initial value and indicates that it has not been changed. Later a user might change the field in validation and the record is updated. This bug causes these updates to not update the date on the record. Thus KAFTA never knows that the data has been updated.

answer:
It is not a requirement to operate KTA via a proxy server. And as such, the information provided in this document is meant to provide general guidance only.
There may be other considerations which you may need to discuss with your local IT team.
Below you will find general guidance on the considerations to be given to the configuration in order to facilitate the communications.
1. Whitelist the following URL's on the proxy:
https://licenseapi.kofax.com/Service.svc
https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v4/token
https://vision.googleapis.com/v1/images:annotate
2. Setup Transformation Service & Clarity extraction to use proxy
a)
Add adapted
system.net
section similar to below to both config files
For Transformation Designer (if installed):
C:\Program Files (x86)\Kofax\TotalAgility\Transformation Designer\Kofax.ProjectBuilder.exe.config
For Transformation Server:
C:\Program Files\Kofax\TotalAgility\Transformation Server\Kofax.CEBPM.CPUServer.ServiceHost.exe.config
If using
OPMT
C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Kofax\Server\ExtractionProcess.exe.config (This is copied on an from Kofax.CEBPM.CPUServer.ServiceHost.exe.config on an On-Premise system)
<system.net>
<defaultProxy enabled="True" useDefaultCredentials="True">
<proxy bypassonlocal="false" proxyaddress="
http://proxy.domain.com:8080
" />
<bypasslist>
<add address="127\.0\.0\.1" />
<add address="localhost" />
<add address="10\.\d{1,3}\.\d{1,3}\.\d{1,3}" />
<add address="172\.16\.\d{1,3}\.\d{1,3}" />
</bypasslist>
</defaultProxy>
</system.net>
Note:
This was added between the </exceptionHandling> and <unity tags.
* Adapt the proxy address attribute as appropriate for the proxy.
* Adapt bypass list addresses entries as required. You will need to ensure access to other KTA servers bypass the proxy.
* Address attribute values need to be regular expression type format, e.g. periods need to be escaped to \.
b)
Add *system* environment variable:
Variable Name = HTTPS_PROXY
Value =
http://proxy.domain.com:8080
Adapt value as appropriate for the proxy.
(If required adapt the values for the HTTP_PROXY system environment variable similarly.)
3.
Restart the transformation service and IISRESET.
4.
Test runtime Clarity extraction.
Note: In an environment where there are multiple Transformation Servers you will need to configure each Transformation Server as required.
If the customer requires further assistance they should reach out to Microsoft Support for further guidance into configuring a proxy server.
Level of Complexity
Moderate

The .NET Assembly for a store must have a strong name and a different assembly version each time it is updated in the store.
The assembly version must be set to auto-increment. To do so follow the steps below.
Open Visual Studio and open the solution.
Select the project.
Right-click the selected project and click
Properties
>
Application
>
Assembly Information
.
In the
Assembly version
field, enter the version. For example, to start version, enter "1.0.*".
Alternatively, you can also set the version in the assemblyinfo.cs file.
Click
OK
.
The assembly will also need to be signed by using the
Signing
tab in a project's
Properties
dialog box in Visual Studio.
Product documentation has incorrectly suggested setting the version to "1.0.0.*" and this has been reported as a bug. For correct behavior with version numbers, it is important that the asterisk is place of the third number as shown above, not the fourth. Explanations and additional information:
c# - How to have an auto incrementing version number (Visual Studio)? - Stack Overflow
AssemblyVersionAttribute Class (System.Reflection) | Microsoft Learn
Reported as Bug 2168655:Incorrect instructions for Auto incrementing the assembly version
Once the DLL properties have been set to the above, any new version of the DLL uploaded to the store will be automatically picked up at runtime.

answer:
Transition to Parascript engine
Starting with KTA 7.10 and KTM 7.0 the A2iA engine has been replaced with the Parascript engine.
The A2iA CheckReader functionality of the A2iA locator has been replaced with a new Parascript Check locator. When opening a project containing an A2iA CheckReader configuration, the locator will be converted into the new Parascript locator.
A2iA FieldReader functionality of the A2iA locator has been replaced with Parascript zonal recognition profiles for reading handwriting, which can be used in an Advanced Zone Locator like any other recognition profile. When opening a project containing an A2iA FieldReader configuration, the locator will be converted into an AZL configured to use the Parascript zonal profile.
In both cases this process will configure Parascript as closely as possible to the original A2iA configurations, however because some functionality differs between the two engines, manual testing and adjustment will be necessary.
Note that the A2iA engine remains in the product, which will allow for comparing existing A2iA configurations to new Parascript configurations.
For further reference a short demonstration is available in
Kofax Learning Cloud
.
See Also:
Parascript/A2iA Feature Compatibility
Level of Complexity
Moderate

answer:
TotalAgility 8.0 and later
In TotalAgility 8.0 and higher, field retention no longer exists as a separate concept. See
TotalAgility Reporting Document Data Retention.
TotalAgility 7.11 and earlier
Reporting on fields can add up to a significant amount of data at scale, which it is why it is important to have a retention period to limit how long the data is kept. By default, KTA reporting data related to fields is configured to only be retained for 5 days. By contrast,
document level reporting data
is configured by default to be retained for 3650 days (ten years).
These settings can be adjusted in the KTA Designer under System > System Settings > Database, retention, and reporting > Reporting Server > Data retention period.
For KTA 7.5 and earlier: System Settings > Settings > General > Reporting > Field Retention Time.
Field Data in KTA Reporting
Which fields are included in reporting?
By default, all fields are included in reporting data. The amount of field data accumulated by reporting can be reduced by unchecking Include in Analytics on individual fields that are not needed for reporting. This option is visible when opening the Extraction Group in the KTA Designer (not Transformation Designer). Preventing unneeded field data from being collected can be beneficial for reducing the amount of data processed by the reporting services, as well as the total amount of data retained in the field_accum_fact table. This can be especially important if there is a need to increase the retention period.
When is data removed if it is older than the Field Retention Period?
At the end of a successful run of the Reporting system task. In other words, when the Reporting service is processing successfully. The Reporting system task defaults to running every minute.
What date is used for retention of field data?
The last reporting session that the field was involved in (field_accum_fact.dt_last_sess). For example, if a validation activity were opened, then that would be a session that included all of the fields of all of the documents in the activity.
What if reporting data is not able to be processed for a duration longer than the Field Retention Period?
When it is eventually able to process successfully, the retention process will happen as normal, meaning that data older than that period would be immediately deleted. For example, this would mean that the data would be deleted before a process like the KAFTA data load would be able to access it.
What Data is Removed
Technically the field data retention process only affects the field_accum_fact table, however because of the
interaction with the document data retention process
, the shorter field data retention period will also affect tables field_dim, field_column_dim, and field_aggregate_fact. So effectively the field retention period affects:
field_accum_fact
field_dim
field_column_dim
field_aggregate_fact
Potential Actions to Take During a Reporting Processing Outage
If reporting data is not able to be processed for a period that will be longer than the Field Retention Period, then this would lead to a gap in field data, because it will be removed by the retention process once processing resumes. If field data is needed, then to avoid a gap you would need to lengthen the Field Retention Period to longer than the duration of the outage,
before allowing successful processing of reporting data
.
For example:
An issue occurs that prevents processing of reporting data (either the service is not running, or it is not processing successfully)
By the time a solution will be available, 7 days will have passed. This is longer than the default Field Retention Period, but the field data has not been deleted yet, because it only happens during successful processing.
Before the solution to the problem is implemented increase the Field Retention Period To 8 days or more.
Once processing begins, the field data will not be lost because of the longer Field Retention Period.
However, it is simpler to just use a very high value such as 1000 than to try to determine an exact number of days for a particular issue.
Then once the backlog of data is processed, the Field Retention Period can be returned to the intended value, such as the default of 5 days.
Field Data in KAFTA
If field data only exists for the past five days, how are reporting solutions such as KAFTA supposed to report on historical data?
The Field Fact record in the KAFTA project stores the records it loads from the field_accum_fact table, for the timespan of the data load that is being performed. When KAFTAs Hourly Execution Plan runs on a schedule, it executes from Last successful load date to Current time, rounded to the beginning of hours. This means that when it is running on schedule, it is always only storing the most recent hour.
What happens if KAFTAs Hourly Execution Plan is not able to be processed for a duration longer than the Field Retention Period?
When it is eventually able to process successfully, it will only have field data for the duration of the Field Retention Period, leaving a gap for any time longer than that when the execution plan was not able to run.
Potential Actions to Take During an Insight/KAFTA Processing Outage
If KAFTAs Hourly Execution Plan is not able to be processed for a period that will be longer than the Field Retention Period, then this would lead to a gap in field data, because the data older than that will no longer be present. If field data is needed, then to avoid a gap you would need to lengthen the Field Retention Period to longer than the duration of the outage,
before the Field Retention Period has passed since the start of the outage
.
For example:
An issue occurs that prevents processing of KAFTAs Execution Plans and the outage has not been solved after 4 days.
To keep field data and avoid a gap, the default 5 day Field Retention Period need to be increased before the Field Retention Period has passed.
The Field Retention Period needs continue to be increased to be larger than the duration of the outage
Once the execution plans are able to run successfully, the field data will not be lost because of the longer Field Retention Period.
Level of Complexity
Moderate

answer:
The system session ID would change either if it was regenerated in the Designer, or if pointing an existing Integration Server to a new tenant with a different system session ID. There are multiple options for how to make the changes.
Uninstall/Reinstall
When the System Session ID in a Cloud/OPMT system is changed, one option to fix the Integration Server is to simply uninstall/reinstall and provide the updated System Session ID in the installer. If needed, this also lets you provide an updated tenant URL at the same time. Integration Server has no local database and usually has no local configuration (edge case Message Connector settings may be an exception), so uninstall/reinstall is often a good option. Export connectors may need to be reinstalled, but export connector mappings persist in the database.
Separate Utility
Instead of the manual changes needed below, consider using the
Change Integration Server Tenant Utility
.
Config file changes with Config Utility
To make the change using the Config Utility, you would change the System Session ID on the Core Worker tab, then click Save.
Unfortunately, there is a bug such that the utility does not update all of the appropriate config files, and this would leave the system with the following problems:
Export connectors will fail to register
Exporting processes from the IS designer will fail
To resolve this, after using the utility to change the System Session ID, then manually change the SystemSessionId value in the following files:
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\CoreWorkerService\Agility.Server.StreamingService.exe.config
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\Agility.Server.Web\bin\Agility.Server.StreamingService.exe.config
And manually change "DefaultSessionId" in the following file:
C:\Program Files (x86)\Tungsten\TotalAgility\ExportConnectors\bin\RegAscSc.exe.config
Then restart IIS and services.
Manual config file changes
Without using the config utility, you can manually change SystemSessionId in these files:
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\CoreWorkerService\Agility.Server.Core.WorkerService.exe.config
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\CoreWorkerService\Agility.Server.Core.ExportWorker.Host.exe.config
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\CoreWorkerService\Agility.Server.Core.ExportService.exe.config
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\CoreWorkerService\Agility.Server.Core.Executor.exe.config
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\CoreWorkerService\Agility.Server.StreamingService.exe.config
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\Agility.Server.Web\bin\Agility.Server.StreamingService.exe.config
C:\Program Files\Tungsten\TotalAgility\Agility.Server.Web\web.config
And manually change "DefaultSessionId" in the following file:
C:\Program Files (x86)\Tungsten\TotalAgility\ExportConnectors\bin\RegAscSc.exe.config
Then restart IIS and services.
Note: for versions older than 8.1, replace Tungsten with Kofax in the folder paths.

answer:
When testing or troubleshooting KAFTA, it may be necessary to run the Hourly Execution Plan to see updated data without waiting an hour for it to run on its normal schedule.
In most situations, the
From date
should be set to
Last successful load date
, the
To date
should be set to
Future
. Because the To date is in the future, its important to leave unchecked the option named 
Save To date as the date of last successful load.
This set of options loads only new data since it was last run, and does not change the next scheduled data load.
Pitfall:
Beginning of times
can cause data loss
Be aware that using a
From date
set to
Beginning of times
causes Insight to truncate (delete) all data before the data load. This is meant as an optimization when reloading all data from a source database.
However, with KAFTA, the source is the TotalAgility reporting database, and the source data only goes back as far as the reporting retention settings. Thus, this would cause KAFTA to lose all data that does not still exist in the TotalAgility reporting tables. With default reporting retention settings, this would mean losing all field data older than five days on older versions, or all data older than 30 days on TotalAgility 8.0 and higher.
Pitfall:
Current time
will not necessarily load new data
The Hourly Execution plan schedule options has the To Date option set to Current time, rounded to the beginning of Hours. This option applies even when doing a manual data load. For example, if you try to run the hourly plan at 5:30 from
Last successful load date
to
Current time
, it will actually use From 5:00 (the last time the hourly plan ran) To 5:00 (current time rounded down to the hour), and thus not load new data. With the rounded option enabled, only a To date of 6:00 or later would load new data. Rather than setting a specific time, it is easier to just choose the
Future
option.
Pitfall: Not checking 
Save To date as the date of last successful load
can cause data loss in a specific situation
Starting with KAFTA 1.5+ and TotalAgility 8.0+, there is a mechanism to cause TotalAgility reporting to delete the reporting data of a document that is completed and already loaded into KAFTA. If you do a data load with this unchecked and then reporting deletes the data of a document, and then KAFTA tries to load over the same time span, then the data doesn't exist on the reporting side, and would be removed.

answer:
First of all, Technical Support cannot provide specifics for customer specific deployments. Shall customer needs assistance with their upgrade process they should contact Professional Services.
Nevertheless, here are some best practices:
There is no official recommendation regarding whether the OS upgrade should be done before or after a TotalAgility upgrade. What we would recommend is performing any testing on a lower environment before executing the same on production.
There are no set rules regarding any type of software and OS upgrades. See examples below.
In the v7.7 Perquisite Guide, the supported OS is Windows 2008 (and above). The "and above" part is due to the fact Windows OS is viewed as backwards compatible. Therefore, it may make sense for this customer to upgrade the OS first.
For a customer upgrading from v7.11 to v8.0.1 and Windows 2016 to Windows 2019, the order doesn't matter. Both versions of the software are supported on both OS versions.
From the approach above provided (which many other customers use) the TotalAgility software or OS does not actually get upgraded. Instead, the TotalAgility v8.1 components are installed on clean Windows 2022 machines.
We have seen PS use the below high-level steps when performing similar upgrades in the past:
Migrate the TotalAgility databases to a new SQL server
Perform a "Database Only" upgrade to upgrade the databases to v8.1
Create new Windows 2022 servers and install the TotalAgility v8.1 components on the new servers
Configure TotalAgility v8.1 components to use the TotalAgility v8.1 databases
For the last step, it is much simpler to create new machines if the environment is virtual. Of course, the customer is more than welcome to upgrade the OS on existing machines if this is what they prefer.
question: Best practice when upgrading KTA and OS

The Web Capture control does not support capture on a mobile or tablet device.

Local path on the Web server

All annotation-related buttons (annotations, sticky note, permanent redaction, mask)

question: Allocate work

answer:
Configure the 
 TotalAgility Workspace
 to automatically allocate work to the right resource by calling the PerformAutoWorkAllocation() API on the ActivityService.
 As needed, call this API from a process that you schedule to run at regular intervals.
The System assigns activities having the Awaiting Allocation status to resources with:
Required auto allocation debits where the due dates fall within the specified date range. See
Manage time categories
for information on debits and credits.
Required skill level.
If you supply due dates (start date/end date), the time required is ignored and replaced with beginning of day/end of day.
 For example, if a business day begins at 9.00 AM and ends at 5.30 PM and you have an activity that changes to the Awaiting
 Allocation status at 2.00 PM of March 15, and remains so until 12.00 AM of March 16, then the due date becomes March 16 (ignoring
 the end time). 
 TotalAgility Workspace
 considers all the resources matching the skill level on that date for automatic allocation of work.
If more than one resource with the required auto allocation debits is available, 
 TotalAgility Workspace
 assigns the resource with the highest skill level to the activity.
Define skill levels at the Process or Server level, depending on your requirement.
Once an activity is allocated, the status changes from Awaiting Allocation to Pending.

Metadata stores supplementary information against Work queue control, work type, job query and more, similar to how file
 properties associated with an activity are stored.
The following example describes the procedure of creating metadata for a job query.
On the
Jobs
menu, click
Find

Expand

On the
Meta Field
list, select a field, such as Date of Joining.
On the
Operator
list, select an operator, such as Equals.
In the
Value
box, enter a value, such as 3/30/2021.
You can use the Like and Not Like operators in your search criteria, and use wildcard characters to search
 on case references and metadata of type String.
TotalAgility
 supports the following wildcard functions.
?
Matches any single character. For example, Bl?ck finds Black or Block.
*
Matches one or more characters. For example, "North*" finds "North", "Northeast", and "Northwest".
And "(*)" finds any content enclosed by parentheses.
~
Used as an escape character to include a literal question mark, asterisk, or tilde. For example:
To find "West*" use "West~*"
To find "West?" use "West~?"
To find "West~" use "West~~"
To find "West~?" use "West~~?"
For a Date field type, the default value is @Today, where Today refers to the start of the day. You can also
 provide a value to be a certain number of days from today in the following format:
@Today +/- NUM. For example, <@Today + 10>, <@Today - 7>

Create

answer:
You can enable logging of business rule history to help debug the rule and make necessary changes.
Enabling logging helps view the business rule history for the business rules executed using a process or form. When a
 business rule is executed within a process with logging status enabled, the
history
is recorded
 as a separate job and can be viewed from the parent job properties by selecting the rule and drilling into the properties. When a
 business rule is executed using a form, the test business rule option or a capture action with logging status enabled, the history
 is recorded as a separate job, and you can search for the business rule job using a job search.
The business rule history is recorded as and when each business rule activity is executed (asynchronous logging) or when
 the business rule within the process is completely executed (synchronous logging). The executing business rule activities appear
 with the history status as 
 "New Business Rule" and the completed business rule activities appear with status 
 "Performed".
You can delete the business rule jobs (delete logging) from the database.
When Archived Finished Job server setting is set to Yes, on completion or termination of parent job, all the associated
 business rule jobs are not moved to archive database.
Export and Import are not supported for business rule logging.
On the
Administration
menu, click
Manage Business Rule Logging
.
The
Manage Business Rule Logging
page displays the list of available business rules.
The
Category
list displays the categories to which the user has access to. By
 default, the working category of the logged-on resource appears on the Category list. To filter the business rule on category, on
 the Category list, select the category to which the business rule belongs.
Update logging status
You can enable logging for a business rule and update its logging status.
Select the check box for the business rule and on the
Logging Status
list, select either option: (Default:
Disabled
)
Record on Rule Completion
: Records the history when the business
 rule is executed.
Record during Rule
: Records the history as and when each business
 rule activity is completed.
Click
Update Status
.
The logging status of the selected business rules is updated.
When a business rule is executed with logging status enabled, all the child rules are also logged. However,
 the child rule logging status does not change.
When a business rule fails, the business rule job and the parent job are suspended.
Delete logs
You can delete logging for a single business rule, multiple business rules or all business rules.
Select the rules:
To delete log for a single rule, select the check box for that rule.
To delete logs for multiple rules, select the check boxes for the rules.
To delete logs for all rules, select the check box on the table header.
Click
Delete Logs
.
When prompted, click
OK
to confirm deletion.
All the business rule jobs and sub jobs associated with business rule jobs are deleted.
The business rule logs are deleted asynchronously.
When you view the history of a business rule for which the logging is deleted for a parent map, a warning
 message appears.

 set working/non-working days and working hours across all resources.
Resources can also have a personal calendar (that is derived from the global calendar) where they can configure the
 following settings for themselves, their team members, and their company:
Daily / custom working hours:
A particular resource may work on Saturday mornings, which is not a working day
 in your company.
Custom debits / credits:
A resource may be on owed time or be on loan (credited) to another team, for example,
 to meet project deadlines.
Configure the global calendar
The global calendar is available to all resources in an organization.
On the
Resources
menu, click
Resource Availability
.
The global calendar appears by default.
Specify the required days or dates:
To select one date and time, select
Select Date
.
To select multiple dates, click
Select Dates
.
To specify non-working days, select
Set Non-Working Dates
.
Browse through the dates on the calendar.
A legend at the bottom of the page indicates the color keys.
Configure the daily working hours of your company as follows:
Select the relevant dates, which enables the
Use Custom
option.
Select the
Use Custom
check box, which enables the
From
and
To
date boxes.
In the
From
and
To
fields, select a start and end time, as required.
Click
Apply
.
To return to the calendar view, click
View Calendar
.
Configure a calendar for an individual
To configure a calendar for yourself or a resource, perform the following steps.
Select the resource from the
Resource
list.
The calendar for the resource appears with two additional options,
Debits and Credits
and
Comments
on the right.
Because these options are only resource-related, they are not available for the global calendar.
Specify whether to loan the resource to another team (debit time) or if the resource is due overtime (credit time)
 by clicking the
Debits and Credits
section.
Existing debits and credits for the resource appear in the
Debits and Credits
list. Delete these if required.
On the
Debit or Credit
list, select the time category.
Time categories are predefined under
Resources
-
Time Categories
. See
Managing time categories
for more information.
Applying more debit hours than working hours generates an error message.
If you select a credit time to loan the resource to another group for a specified time, from the
Resource Group
list, select the group that is borrowing the resource.
Enter the duration of the credit / debit time in the
Duration
boxes.
Select a date from the Calendar.
Click
Add
.
The new debit/credit appears in the
Debit/Credit
list.
Add debits and credits for non-working days as needed. You can add the same debit /credit durations multiple times
 to the
Debit/Credit
list and have duplicate debit and credit entries.
If you have a work pattern defined, apply debits and credits to the work pattern by selecting the
Apply Work Pattern
check box. (By default, the check box is enabled.)
For more information about work patterns, see the topics that follow.
To add a comment to a date:
Select the required date and click
Comments
.
Enter the comment text and click
Apply
.
Once you finish working with your resources calendar, click
Save Changes to Calendar
to update the calendar.
Add work patterns
For an individual resource, you can add work patterns that allow you to apply debits or credits.
When you move the pointer over a date, the Work Pattern Debits/Credits appear along with the Custom Debits / Credits
 details.
Click
View Work Pattern
.
On the
Resource
list, select a resource and click

Create
.
The
Work Pattern
dialog box appears.
In the
Pattern Name
box, enter a name for the work pattern.
Select the
Default
check box to make the pattern the default.
Specify a start and end date by selecting the check box and selecting the dates to use.
Except for the default work pattern, you must select a start and end date for all work patterns.
Do not define a date range that overlaps the ranges of other work patterns. However, work patterns can overlap the
 default work pattern.
Click
Submit
. The new pattern is created and displayed in the
Work Pattern
list.
Add other work patterns, as needed.
Click
Save Changes
.
Maintain work patterns
To view details of the weekly work pattern, click
View Work Pattern
.
To change the current work pattern to a different one, on the
Work Pattern
list, select the work pattern to use.
To edit a work pattern, select the work pattern and click

Edit
.
To modify the weekly work pattern for the global calendar or a selected resource:
View the details of the weekly work pattern by clicking
View Work Pattern
.
On the
Work Pattern
list, select the global or individual resource to modify.
For the global calendar, you can only modify the working hours. For resources, you can modify the Debits / Credits
 time for a particular day, and add comments.
Modify the work pattern details as needed.
Click
Save Changes to Calendar
to update the calendar.

question: Manage resource availability

answer:
A resource is a person, or a group of persons who perform tasks in an organization. You can manage groups and individuals,
 and add external users to your system.
To learn more about using 
 TotalAgility Workspace
 to create and effectively manage your resources, click the following links:
Create a
 user
Create
 a group
Create an external user
View groups associated with a user, group, or external user
Search for a resource
Modify a resource

question: Manage resources

answer:
A security role is a role that has users and a security token assigned to it.
You create a security token and associate it with a form control in 
 TotalAgility Designer. Refer to the
Kofax TotalAgility Designer
 Help
for more information on security tokens.
Then within the Workspace, administrators can create security roles and associate tokens and users with the security
 roles, thus delivering flexibility in how security is managed.
On the
Administration
menu, click
Security Roles
.
The
Security Roles
page appears.
Click

Create
.
The
Security Role
-
Create
page appears.
Select a category, and enter a name and description for the security role.
Associate tokens with the security role:
Click the
Tokens
tab.
Click

Add
.
The
Security Tokens
dialog box appears.
Select the security tokens to associate with the role.
Associate users with a security role:
Click the
Resources
tab.
Select users or groups.
Click
Save
.
The new security role is saved with tokens and associated users or groups.
